summary: Matched the Kirby hammer item translation unit by replacing asm/melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.s with C in src/melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.c, switching build configuration from NonMatching/asm to Matching/C, and adding the minimal item-var typing needed for the hammer state. The PR also adjusted PAD_STACK so stack padding can be emitted without zero-initialization, likely to preserve the matched stack/codegen shape.
what_changed: Implemented src/melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.c with the item state table it_803F6C98 and functions it_802ADC04, it_802ADC34, it_802ADC54, it_802ADD88, and it_802ADDB0.
it_802ADC04 now expresses the owner-null guard in C and calls ftKb_SpecialAirLw_800F539C when the hammer has an owner.

it_802ADC54 now constructs a SpawnItem for It_Kind_Kirby_Hammer, copies prev_pos from the input position, derives spawn.pos with it_8026BB68, sets facing direction, zeroes velocity and damage, sets parent pointers, sets x44_flag.bits.b0, then calls Item_80268B18.

Extracted the particle/effect setup into a static helper named setupHammerParticles, which stores the passed vars into item xDD4_itemVar.kirbyhammer.x0, calls db_80225DD8 and Item_8026AB54, chooses particle ID 1177 when the stored value is 1 and 1176 otherwise, gets the item JObj via it_80272C90, and calls efAsync_Spawn at item xBC0.
Updated itkirbyhammer.h from address-comment prototypes and old include guard style to typed prototypes using HSD_GObj, Vec3, u8, u32, and f32.
Added itKirbyHammerVars with a single u32 x0 field to src/melee/it/itCharItems.h and added it as kirbyhammer in Item.xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Changed configure.py to mark melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.c as Matching.
Changed obj_files.mk to build src/melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.c.o instead of asm/melee/it/items/itkirbyhammer.s.o.
Changed PAD_STACK(bytes) in src/placeholder.h from a zero-initialized unused byte array to an uninitialized unused byte array.
smart_moves: Kept unknown item-specific data minimal by naming the new union member kirbyhammer and giving it only the observed u32 x0 field rather than inventing a larger struct.
Used a static helper, setupHammerParticles, to isolate the post-spawn side effects and particle-ID branch, making the spawn function map cleanly to the original assembly structure.
Preserved matching-sensitive stack layout in it_802ADC54 with PAD_STACK(12) instead of forcing unrelated local variables or fake logic.
Promoted the file through both build systems: configure.py changed to Object(Matching, ...) and obj_files.mk changed from asm object to C object.
Represented the spawn parameter block with existing SpawnItem fields and bitfield access such as x44_flag.bits.b0 instead of raw stack offsets.
Used existing item helpers and accessors such as GET_ITEM, Item_80268B18, Item_80268E5C, it_8026B894, and it_80272C90 to keep the C implementation aligned with project conventions.
decomp_lessons: For item matches, moving a file from asm to C requires synchronized changes in both configure.py and obj_files.mk; missing either leaves the build using the wrong object.
Small per-item state stored in Item.xDD4_itemVar should be modeled through a typed union member in it/types.h, with the backing struct placed in itCharItems.h when it is character-item related.
When a function builds a SpawnItem on the stack, matching may require explicit padding; PAD_STACK is a project tactic for matching stack frame shape.
Zero-initializing artificial padding can change generated code. This PR changed PAD_STACK to leave the padding array uninitialized, which is important for future stack-padding matches.
Do not over-name unknown item var fields. A neutral field name like x0 is acceptable when evidence only shows a u32 value used to choose between two particle IDs.
Effect-spawn code can often be recovered by identifying the item JObj source, item position field, particle ID constants, and parent/linking helper calls around efAsync_Spawn.
naming_conventions: File-local helper was named in descriptive lowerCamelCase: setupHammerParticles.
The item-specific variable struct was named itKirbyHammerVars, following the item name but retaining a lowercase initial 'it' prefix.
The Item union member was named kirbyhammer, matching the item file/name in lowercase.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names, such as itKirbyHammerVars.x0 and Item.xDD4_itemVar.
Matched functions retained address-based symbol names such as it_802ADC04, it_802ADC54, it_802ADD88, and it_802ADDB0.
The header include guard changed to _itkirbyhammer_h_, consistent with some project item header style visible in the diff.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the original assembly once the C matched and pointed obj_files.mk at the C object.
Used PAD_STACK(12) in it_802ADC54 to shape the stack frame for the SpawnItem construction.
Changed PAD_STACK to allocate an uninitialized unused byte array rather than a zero-filled one, avoiding extra stores that would interfere with matching.
Used chained assignment for zeroing spawn.vel.x, spawn.vel.y, and spawn.vel.z to express the original zero stores compactly.
Kept the particle choice as integer constants 1177 and 1176, matching the assembly constants 0x499 and 0x498.
Used explicit casts between Item_GObj*, HSD_GObj*, and ftKb_GObj* where needed to match existing APIs and inferred runtime object types.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename itKirbyHammerVars.x0 or document the vars argument to it_802ADC54.
Search for other PRs that rely on the uninitialized PAD_STACK change in src/placeholder.h.
Compare other character item files that set Item.xDD4_itemVar and spawn effects through efAsync_Spawn.
Check whether it_802ADC54 callers reveal the meaning of vars values 0 and 1 or particle IDs 1176 and 1177.
